mechanism_narrative: Monthly retail sales (RSAFS) rising as a trend signals robust consumer demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. This is an always-on trend-following signal that does not require a threshold condition.
failure_condition: If the rolling 2-year rank correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is non-positive, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, monetary policy is restrictive and further increases compress equity valuations via higher discount rates, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns. The signal is triggered only above the threshold.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%, the proportion of positive 5-day SPX returns exceeds 50%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad-based economic expansion with rising output and employment, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive equity returns over the next month. The joint behavior is more informative than either series alone.
failure_condition: If the joint dummy (both series rising month-over-month) does not yield a mean positive 20-day return of at least 0.5% with t-test p<0.05 over any 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is already below 4.0%, a further decline indicates an overheating labor market that forces the Fed to tighten, compressing equity valuations over the next quarter. The signal operates only in the very low unemployment regime.
failure_condition: If in periods with UNRATE below 4.0%, the rank correlation between UNRATE declines and subsequent 60-day returns is non-negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly surge in CPI of more than 0.5% signals accelerating inflation that forces the Fed to hike rates quickly, causing a short-term negative market reaction. The threshold identifies episodes of acute inflation pressure.
failure_condition: If following such CPI surges, the median 5-day SPX return is positive or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1 rising) indicates overall economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter. This is a fundamental always-on trend signal using the broadest output measure.
failure_condition: If the rolling 5-year rank correlation between quarter-over-quarter GDP growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not positive (<=0), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising and the unemployment rate is rising concurrently, the economy faces stagflationary conditions that hurt both earnings (via higher costs and weak demand) and valuations (via tighter policy), producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the joint dummy (both series rising month-over-month) does not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences over any 3-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS above 5.0%), a decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening real estate demand and economic slowdown, which reduces earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If in periods with FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, the rank correlation between HOUST declines and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not negative (rank IC >= 0) over any 3-year window, reject.
